questions.option_b: I'd prefer to monitor the growth with regular check-ups rather than have unnecessary surgery right now.

questions.scenario: You're considering whether to invest in your friend's food truck business, which would require most of your savings but could provide steady returns.
questions.option_a: I'm going to invest in their food truck because I believe in their culinary skills and business plan.
questions.option_b: I'll keep my savings in safer investments and maybe contribute a smaller amount I can afford to lose.

questions.scenario: A dance instructor at a cultural festival invites audience members to join in learning traditional folk dances you've never seen before.
questions.option_a: I'll appreciate the performance from the audience rather than potentially embarrassing myself trying to follow along.
questions.option_b: I'll join in and try to learn the folk dances – it's a great cultural experience even if I look awkward.

questions.scenario: You discover that a popular social media influencer you follow has been promoting products using misleading health claims.
questions.option_a: I'll unfollow them and maybe report the content, but I won't get involved in public confrontation.
questions.option_b: I'm going to call out these misleading claims publicly since people's health decisions could be affected.

questions.scenario: Your teenager wants to take a gap year before college to work with a environmental conservation group in Costa Rica.
questions.option_a: I think they should go straight to college while they're in academic mode and save travel experiences for summer breaks.
questions.option_b: I'll support their gap year plan because the international experience could be more educational than rushing into college.

questions.scenario: You're offered the chance to beta test a new virtual reality game that's still in development and might have some glitches.
questions.option_a: I'd love to beta test the VR game – it's exciting to experience cutting-edge technology even if it's not perfect yet.
questions.option_b: I'll wait until the game is officially released and the bugs are worked out before trying it.

questions.scenario: A local newspaper wants to interview you about your controversial opinion on a community development project.
questions.option_a: I'd rather not be quoted in the newspaper since my views might create conflict with neighbors and colleagues.
questions.option_b: I'll do the interview because it's important that different perspectives on this development project are heard publicly.

questions.scenario: You're considering whether to drive through a mountain pass during winter weather when the forecast shows possible snow conditions.
questions.option_a: I'll take the longer route around the mountains to avoid potentially dangerous winter driving conditions.
questions.option_b: I'll make the drive through the pass since I have good tires and emergency supplies in case conditions worsen.

questions.scenario: A friend invites you to join their weekend pottery class where you'll be working with a kiln and potentially dangerous equipment you've never used.
questions.option_a: I think I should start with a beginner class that focuses on basic techniques before working with kilns and advanced tools.
questions.option_b: I'll join the pottery class – I'm sure the instructor will teach us proper safety procedures for using the equipment.

questions.scenario: You're thinking about confronting a family member who consistently makes inappropriate jokes at gatherings, even though it might cause family drama.
questions.option_a: I'm going to have a private conversation with them about how their jokes make people uncomfortable, despite potential conflict.
questions.option_b: I'll continue to change the subject when they make inappropriate jokes rather than risk causing family tension.

questions.scenario: A real estate agent suggests you make an offer on a house that's at the top of your budget but in a rapidly appreciating neighborhood.
questions.option_a: I'll make an offer at my maximum budget because the potential appreciation could make it a great long-term investment.
questions.option_b: I should look for something more affordable that leaves room in my budget for unexpected expenses and market changes.

questions.scenario: You're invited to participate in a clinical research study for a new allergy medication that could help your symptoms.
questions.option_a: I'll volunteer for the clinical study since it could help my allergies and contribute to medical research.
questions.option_b: I'll stick with my current allergy management and let others participate in the research study first.

questions.scenario: Your boss asks if you want to lead a high-visibility project with an extremely tight deadline that could advance your career significantly.
questions.option_a: I'll take on the high-visibility project because the career advancement opportunity outweighs the stress of the tight deadline.
questions.option_b: I'll respectfully decline since the timeline seems unrealistic and failure could damage my reputation more than success would help.

questions.scenario: A local adventure company offers discounted bungee jumping lessons for first-time jumpers at a nearby bridge.
questions.option_a: I'm going to sign up for the bungee jumping – it's something I've always wondered about trying.
questions.option_b: I'll pass on the bungee jumping and find a less extreme way to challenge myself physically.

questions.scenario: You're considering whether to speak up during a town hall meeting about a proposed policy change that you strongly disagree with.
questions.option_a: I'll submit my comments in writing rather than speaking publicly, since I'm not comfortable with that kind of attention.
questions.option_b: I'm going to speak during the public comment period because citizens need to voice their concerns about local policies.

questions.scenario: A friend offers you the chance to join their amateur skydiving group for a tandem jump with certified instructors.
questions.option_a: I'll go skydiving with them – the safety record for tandem jumps is excellent and it would be an incredible experience.
questions.option_b: I appreciate the invitation, but jumping out of an airplane isn't something I'm comfortable doing regardless of safety measures.

questions.scenario: You're thinking about switching your child to a new experimental education program that uses innovative teaching methods but has no track record yet.
questions.option_a: I'll enroll them in the experimental program because innovative approaches to education could give them unique advantages.
questions.option_b: I think it's better to keep them in the established program with proven results rather than experimenting with their education.

questions.scenario: A cryptocurrency exchange is offering bonus tokens for early adopters of their new digital wallet system that launches next month.
questions.option_a: I'm going to sign up early for the bonus tokens since getting in early on new crypto platforms can be very profitable.
questions.option_b: I'll wait to see how the platform performs after launch before trusting them with my cryptocurrency investments.

questions.scenario: You notice smoke coming from a neighboring apartment and hear what might be someone calling for help, but you're not completely sure.
questions.option_a: I'm going to knock on their door to check if they need help while someone else calls the fire department.
questions.option_b: I'll immediately call the fire department and emergency services rather than potentially putting myself in danger.
